titleStmt.title: SII 31.179: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 179. (A.R. No. B 347 of 1938.) MĀDĀLA, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: Pillar lying in the temple of Sagarēśvarasvāmi.
It is dated Śaka 1184, Uttarāyaṇasankrānti, corresponding to 1263 A.D.

Registers the gift of three māḍas of pa[ṭa]mu (Polamu ?) and fifty sheep for maintaining the perpetual lamp (akahaṇḍavatti-dīpa) in the temple of Sakalēśvara at Māvuṁdala (Mādāla) by Ponikaṁṭi Dēvaya bhaḷumḍu. Dēvayabhaḷu is described as the faithful servant (Ishṭa-bhṛityuḍu) of Mahāpradhāni Bētana preggaḍa.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti . śakava¨rṣaṁbulu 1185 gu nēṁṭṭi
Uttarāyana saṁkrāṁtti nimityamuna śrī
mannamahāpratā(dhā)ni bētanapreggaḍaṁ gāriki niṣṭha
bhrityuṁḍaina śrīmatuponikaṁṭi dēvayabha
ḷuṁḍu damatalli taṁḍriki dha¨rmmātta[¨rddha]mugānu
māvuṁdala śrīsakalēśvara mahādēvaraku
mūṁḍu māḍalapalamupolamusama¨rppaṇasēsi A
khaṁḍavatti dīpaṁbunakiccina goṟeyaE (goṟiyalu) 55 vī
niṁjēkoni pōlabōyuni koḍku vēmanabōyuṁḍu ta
na putrānu pautrikamācaṁdrā¨rkamugā dīpa
ṁbunaku nityamāneṁḍu nēyi vōyaṁgala
vāḍu .
Digital edition of SII 31.179 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
